k is a key variable to track the index while solving this array problem with special rules for every third element k appears again here to mark the pattern shift from absolute differences to squared differences when the index is divisible by three

***
the solution uses prefix sums to calculate the total distance efficiently for standard indices but switches to a formula involving squares of values for those special positions where the index k is a multiple of three so we can handle large inputs within the time limit without using commas or capital letters in this text
